name: ZGC — Colored Pointers and Self-Healing Load Barriers
summary: OpenJDK's ZGC is a concurrent region-based compacting collector using colored 64-bit pointers with metadata bits and load barriers to translate stale references on the fly, achieving sub-millisecond pauses independent of heap size up to 16 TB.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
OpenJDK's **ZGC** is a concurrent region-based compacting collector using **colored 64-bit pointers** with metadata bits and **load barriers** to translate stale references on the fly, achieving sub-millisecond pauses independent of heap size up to 16 TB.

ZGC and Shenandoah are the two production heirs to Azul's C4 lineage; both inherit the colored-pointer/load-barrier design but diverge on barrier choice and remembered-set strategy.

sections.title: Colored pointers and self-healing load barriers
sections.content:
**Colored pointers** — Marked0/Marked1/Remapped/Finalizable bits embedded in the high bits of 64-bit references; a load barrier resolves stale pointers without an STW remap phase. **Self-healing barriers** update the loaded reference back into the field, ensuring each barrier slow path fires at most once per location.

The self-healing property is what gives ZGC its constant-time barrier behaviour: the slow path runs once per field, then subsequent loads from the healed field hit the fast path. This is the key correctness/performance invariant ZGC inherits from Azul C4's Loaded Value Barrier.

sections.title: Generational ZGC (JEP 439)
sections.content:
**Generational ZGC** (JEP 439, JDK 21+) added a young generation with store barriers for young/old tracking; in JDK 25 it became the *only* ZGC implementation (legacy non-generational mode removed).

The rationale: even pauseless concurrent compaction benefits from generational filtering of long-lived from short-lived data. Most allocations are short-lived (the generational hypothesis); concentrating GC work on the young generation reduces the total marking and compaction effort, while the colored-pointer + load-barrier infrastructure ensures pauses remain sub-millisecond regardless.

sections.title: Status and deployment
sections.content:
Status (as of 2026-04, JDK 25): production since JDK 15. **Generational ZGC is the only ZGC mode in JDK 25 LTS**, which does not imply ZGC is the default JVM collector — G1 remains the default; ZGC is opt-in for latency-sensitive workloads. Linux/Windows/macOS.

With Project Lilliput (compact object headers, JEP 519) shipped in JDK 25, ZGC's barrier costs scale proportionally — fewer object headers traversed means lower steady-state barrier overhead.
